extra_model_fields.expected_effect: Removes automated verification blocker; increases transparency and reduces reviewer friction.
extra_model_fields.expected_time_estimate: 1-2 hours (mostly workbook edits and local validation)
post_revision_checks: Open the revised XLSX with openpyxl in CI or Excel to confirm parse_status != openpyxl_unavailable and verify FORMULATEXT presence where used.
Confirm the numeric outputs in actual_outputs still match trace step outputs after replacing cached values with formulas (re-run simulation checks).
Re-run verifier to ensure verifier_issue_count becomes 0.

planned_changes: Open the baseline workbook and identify any cells that contain cached values rather than formulas.

Replace cached result cells with live formulas that reference the authoritative input cells or named ranges used in the model (safetyStock formulas, daily inflow/outflow calculations, projection tables, unmet demand calculations).
Add a visible 'Traceability & Checks' block (single cell: 'Inputs traceable = TRUE' and short mapping of input cells to source values) on the Inputs sheet.
Add a per-day mass-balance validation table (plant outflow = sum(DC inflows) and plant inventory change) with pass/fail flags so reviewers can immediately confirm conservation.
Expose formula text alongside key outputs (e.g., use FORMULATEXT or a separate notes column) or create named ranges for all key input groups so formulas are easily auditable.
Enhance the mitigation/decision sheet to include exact trigger cell references and a before/after numeric column quantifying expected benefit for each recommendation.
Create at least two dedicated scenario tabs fully wired with formulas to reproduce scenarioNotes sensitivity cases, and link a scenario selector cell to toggle recalculation where possible.
Run local openpyxl-based parser or open in Excel to confirm all formulas are present and save the revised workbook.
Update the attempt to 186-revised, attach the revised workbook, and re-run the grader/verifier to clear the parsing limitation.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Automated graders/verifiers could not parse the XLSX (openpyxl unavailable) and builder metadata indicates cached formulas were used. Convert cached values to live formulas and add transparency/check elements so automated validation and manual reviewers can verify calculations.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt passed all rubric checks and received a final score of 1.0. Grader and verifier agree numeric outputs (safety stocks, day14/day21 inventories, plantEnd, unmet demand, scenarioNotes) are consistent with the simulation described in the trace. The only outstanding issue is that the environment could not parse the delivered XLSX (openpyxl_unavailable) and the workbook builder metadata indicates cached formulas were used; this prevents automated verification that formula cells are visible (not cached values). A manual workbook inspection or a rebuild removing cached single-cell values is recommended before final publication.
